politics.name: Politics
politics.summary: Intra-party rift over emerging Iran peace deal: The Trump administration's tentative deal to end the Iran war is facing fierce backlash from conservative allies and former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo, triggering aggressive White House pushback and signaling deep Republican divisions over foreign policy. Escalating security concerns in Washington: A shooting incident outside the White House has renewed focus on executive security infrastructure, while the debut of highly advanced AI models (such as GPT-5.5 and Mythos) with powerful hacking capabilities is jolting federal policymakers over cyber vulnerability.

articles.title: Live coverage: Trump's tentative Iran deal faces GOP criticism; Shooting at White House renews security fears
articles.summary: President Trump on Saturday said a deal with Iran to end the war is getting closer, but a number of Republicans and other conservatives said they feared the emerging agreement was not a good one for the United States. The emerging deal is likely to take center stage on the Sunday talk shows over Mem

articles.title: Trump thanks Secret Service, renews call for ballroom after White House shooting
articles.summary: President Trump early Sunday thanked the Secret Service for their actions in neutralizing an armed man who exchanged shots with officers outside the White House on Saturday and argued that the incident shows the importance of the under-construction White House ballroom. “Thank you to our great Secre

articles.title: White House says ex-Secretary of State Pompeo should ‘shut his stupid mouth’ on Iran
articles.summary: White House Communications Director Stephen Cheung blasted former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo for criticizing a deal being negotiated by the Trump administration to end the Iran war, telling the one-time Trump ally to “shut his stupid mouth.” Cheung wrote that Pompeo “has no idea what the f--- he

articles.title: What to know about the AI models that are jolting Washington
articles.summary: Researchers who have tested Anthropic’s Mythos and OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 say their hacking capabilities are a “game-changer.”

global_events.name: State of the World & Global Events
global_events.summary: In Bolivia, escalating civil unrest over economic policies led to an armed ambush on a government minister’s convoy during roadblock clearances, while a suspected Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has claimed the lives of Red Cross volunteers. A major rescue operation successfully retrieved 24 survivors from a building collapse in the Philippines, while in the United States, lawmakers and religious groups are lobbying for $1 billion to address a critical security funding shortage for houses of worship.

articles.title: Religious leaders, lawmakers push for $1 billion to secure houses of worship
articles.summary: There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.

articles.title: People trapped under collapsed building in Philippines
articles.summary: A search operation has so far rescued 24 people with no deaths reported at the site, in a city near the capital Manila.

articles.title: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
articles.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

articles.title: Red Cross volunteers die from suspected Ebola in DR Congo
articles.summary: They are thought to have caught the virus before the outbreak was identified, the Red Cross says.

corporate_strategy.name: Industry & Corporate Strategy
corporate_strategy.summary: Shifting Consumer & Capital Demands: Industries are adapting to major market pivots, highlighted by Gen Z driving a beverage sector shift from hard seltzers to non-carbonated drinks, and venture capitalists aggressively funding India’s booming rooftop solar sector with deals like SolarSquare's potential $60 million raise. Strategic Technology Integration: Leading global brands are deploying advanced technology to address security and engagement, with tech giants (Apple, Meta, Google) introducing specialized anti-spyware modes, and Ferrari partnering with IBM to leverage AI to revolutionize the fan experience.

articles.title: Move over, seltzer. Non-carbonated drinks are taking the spotlight
articles.summary: Non-carbonated alcoholic drinks like Surfside and BeatBox are stealing "share of throat" from hard seltzers, particularly among Gen Z.

articles.title: SolarSquare in talks to raise up to $60M as India’s rooftop solar market draws major VC interest
articles.summary: SolarSquare could be valued at up to $500 million in the financing expected to close next month.

articles.title: These special phone and app features can help protect you from spyware
articles.summary: Apple, Meta, and Google offer special security modes that provide your devices more secure against targeted spyware attacks. Here are how those modes work, what they do, and how to switch them on.

articles.title: Ferrari is using IBM’s AI to create F1 superfans
articles.summary: IBM and Scuderia Ferrari HP take TechCrunch inside how they are redefining the fan experience.

tech_ai.name: Tech & AI Landscape
tech_ai.summary: Internal accounts shed light on the intense governance crisis that nearly collapsed OpenAI, while emerging cybersecurity threats show hackers are actively learning to exploit AI chatbot "personalities." The autonomous vehicle sector is intensifying as competitors like Nuro attempt to challenge Waymo's market dominance by leveraging "second mover" strategic advantages.

articles.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
articles.summary: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI mischief, follow Robert Hart. articles.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
articles.summary: Waymo is the undisputed leader in the robotaxi space, operating a fleet of over 3,000 driverless cars in at least 10 cities across the US. macro_markets.name: Global Macro & Market Trends
macro_markets.summary: Geopolitical Risk and Insurance Strain: Escalating conflict risks involving Iran are placing significant financial pressure on global insurers, with some firms already suffering heavy losses and others facing imminent exposure to rising war-related liabilities. Global Trade and Housing Indicators: China's overwhelming manufacturing success is crowding out global competitors and reshaping international trade dynamics, while the financial performance of major retailers like Home Depot serves as a key macroeconomic gauge for the health of the U.S. housing market.

culture_shift.name: Culture & Digital Shift
culture_shift.summary: Specialized streaming platforms are accelerating the globalization of regional media, elevating genres like anime into dominant, internationally celebrated cultural forces. The intersection of digital streaming accessibility and cross-platform merchandising (such as physical-digital gaming collaborations) is transforming global sports icons into multifaceted entertainment brands that redefine fan engagement.

articles.title: ‘Everytime’ Review: A Grieving Family Finds a Strange Path Towards Healing in Sandra Wollner’s Poised, Haunting Third Feature
articles.summary: Everyone who has ever experienced serious grief knows the strange, unsettling things it can do to time: stretching it or compressing it by turns, consigning some passages of it to a black hole of memory, or sometimes just suspending it entirely. articles.title: ‘My Hero Academia Final Season’ Lands Top Prize at 10th Annual Crunchyroll Anime Awards
articles.summary: “My Hero Academia Final Season” took home the top prize for Anime of the Year at the 10th Crunchyroll Anime Awards, held on Saturday, May 23, in Tokyo. security_governance.name: Security, Compliance & Governance
security_governance.summary: A contractor for the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) compromised federal infrastructure security by leaking sensitive AWS GovCloud credentials on a public GitHub repository. Researchers successfully utilized Anthropic's Mythos AI model to identify and exploit a critical kernel memory corruption vulnerability in Apple's M5 silicon, highlighting the growing role of AI in advanced exploit development.

articles.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Regulating Squid Fishing in the South Pacific
articles.summary: The South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organization (SPRFMO) needs to regulate squid fishing in the South Pacific. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

articles.summary: Crazy story : Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. articles.title: macOS Kernel Memory Corruption Exploit
articles.summary: A group used Anthropic’s Mythos AI model to help find a kernel memory corruption vulnerability and exploit on Apple’s M5. News article .

articles.summary: When it comes to keeping our texts, chats, and other digital messages safe from prying eyes, we have a powerful tool: end-to-end encryption. applied_science.name: Physical Systems & Applied Science
applied_science.summary: Advanced semiconductor failure analysis and nanofabrication are achieving higher precision through SEM-guided, low-voltage focused ion beam (FIB) finishing techniques designed for delicate TEM lamella preparation. The evolution of physical AI and robotics is shifting focus toward smarter, intuitive control interfaces and autonomous, AI-powered systems designed to seamlessly collaborate with humans in practical environments.

articles.title: Developers: Get Your Medical Mobile App Verified By IEEE
articles.summary: Patients who use mobile applications to manage medical conditions including depression and chronic pain might assume the apps have been evaluated by regulatory agencies to be safe and effective. articles.summary: Over the next few decades, billions of autonomous, AI-powered robots will work alongside people in factories, perform tedious tasks in warehouses, care for the elderly, assist in unsafe disaster areas , deliver packages and food to our doorsteps, and eventually help out in our homes. workplace_finance.name: Workplace, Labor & Personal Finance
workplace_finance.summary: Labor and regulatory updates in the healthcare sector highlight ongoing discussions around licensing midwives and the economic challenges of reopening shuttered rural hospitals. Personal finance and investing trends include scrutiny of political figures purchasing pharmaceutical stocks tied to policy boosts, alongside new medical research identifying unnecessary screenings that could impact healthcare spending for older adults.

articles.summary: Some screenings and treatments no longer make sense for patients as they age. Researchers have just added a few more to the list.

articles.title: Trump’s $50B Rural Health Bet Meets a Healthcare Desert in North Carolina
articles.summary: Republicans promise that $50 billion in new health funding will help rural America. But it’s not expected to aid the years-long effort in North Carolina’s Martin County to reopen its only hospital.
